QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_65

question.text: Which of the following matters is NOT dealt with by SEBI under the SCORES mechanism?

answer.text: Complaints against stockbrokers registered with SEBI
Complaints related to the SEBI Act violations
Complaints against companies that have been wound up
Complaints under Section 26 of the Companies Act

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI does not deal with complaints against companies that have been wound up, as well as unlisted/delisted companies, matters that are sub-judice, and matters falling under other regulators' purview. A wound-up company has no operational existence for SEBI to act upon. **The Trap:** Complaints under the SEBI Act, SCRA, Depositories Act, and Section 26 of the Companies Act are all within SCORES' scope. Only wound-up, delisted, or unlisted company complaints fall outside. **Cross-Exam Context:** The jurisdictional boundaries of SEBI's complaint redressal mechanism are relevant to NISM Series IX and general investor awareness modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Before filing a SCORES complaint, check the company's listing status — if wound up or delisted, pursue remedies through the appropriate forum (NCLT for wound-up companies).
